notes:
dù sao = anyway / in any case. It’s used to say “despite that” or “no matter what happened before”.

It often shows contrast: something negative happened, but the result is still true.  

It softens disappointment or accepts reality, like “well, anyway…”.  
Very close to dẫu sao (a bit more formal, same meaning).
exampleSentences: Anh quên mang ô, nhưng dù sao anh cũng đi làm được.
Chị không thích món này lắm, nhưng dù sao cũng sẽ thử một chút.
Em thấy bài kiểm tra khó, nhưng dù sao cũng cố gắng hết sức.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I forgot to bring an umbrella, but anyway I still got to work.
She doesn't really like this dish, but anyway she will try a little.
I found the test difficult, but anyway I tried my best.
notes_md:
**dù sao** = *anyway / in any case*. It’s used to say “despite that” or “no matter what happened before”.

It often shows contrast: something negative happened, but the result is still true.  

It softens disappointment or accepts reality, like “well, anyway…”.  
Very close to **dẫu sao** (a bit more formal, same meaning).

definitions.vi: Cụm từ dùng để kết nối, thể hiện ý kiến bất chấp những gì đã xảy ra trước đó.
definitions.es: De todos modos; de todas formas.

content.sentence: Anh quên mang ô, nhưng ___ anh cũng đi làm được.

translation.en: I forgot to bring an umbrella, but anyway I still got to work.

content.sentence: Chị không thích món này lắm, nhưng ___ cũng sẽ thử một chút.

translation.en: She doesn't really like this dish, but anyway she will try a little.
